After gathering evidence up to October 26, 2025, the key developments include the rescission of the January AI Diffusion Rule in May, replaced by nonregulatory guidance and strengthened enforcement under existing rules. The July 2025 Executive Order directs the development of full-stack AI export packages, but no formal new or expanded controls were published in the Federal Register or EAR updates during the May-October period. While some sources reference '2025 rules' closing loopholes on cloud access and model weights, the lack of official verification suggests these may be policy shifts or guidance rather than qualifying new implementations. The EO and monitoring indicate potential for future controls, but as of the cutoff, no broad, verifiable expansions with effective dates in 2025 are confirmed beyond the January baseline. Balancing the base rate and evidence, I estimate 40% chance that such controls were implemented by year-end.

Given the Republican trifecta in government under President Trump, who has issued deregulatory executive orders on AI, and the lack of any substantive federal AI regulation bills advancing beyond introduction (e.g., H.R. 5388 is explicitly deregulatory), with focus instead on preempting state laws, the probability of passing qualifying legislation in the remaining ~2 months is very low. Older bipartisan proposals appear stalled, and the slim House majority may hinder ambitious legislation. Aligning with the crowd forecast of 0.01, but bounding at 0.05 due to uncertainty.
